name: Summary Quality
summary: Rates a summary against its source on 5 independent aspects (coherence, consistency, fluency, informativeness, relevance) on a 1–5 scale, one call per aspect.
description: An LLM-as-a-Judge for summarization. Given a source text and a summary of it, it scores the summary across five independent aspects on a 1–5 scale — coherence, consistency (faithfulness to the source), fluency, informativeness, and relevance — making one LLM call per aspect using a single, aspect-agnostic prompt whose placeholders are filled per aspect. It returns a 'score' dict and a 'reasoning' dict, each keyed by aspect. Each aspect is scored independently with no aggregate score. Adapted from HypoEval (Li, Li & Tan, 2025, arXiv:2504.07174), without the per-hypothesis loop.
